findings.message: Command declares required argument <question> but provides no empty-input behaviour; invoking with no args passes empty string to ask.js which exits with a raw usage error.
Phrase 'when applicable' is a vague quantifier with no defined criteria.
Phrase 'when helpful' is a vague quantifier with no defined criteria.
${ARGUMENTS:-20} is unquoted in the bash invocation; word-splitting and glob expansion are active, enabling shell injection if ARGUMENTS contains metacharacters (e.g. /mind:recent $(id)).
Command declares required argument <query> but provides no empty-input behaviour; invoking with no args passes empty string to find.js which exits with a raw usage error.

"$ARGUMENTS" is passed as a single quoted token so <query> and [limit] are concatenated; the hardcoded 10 on the bash line is always used as the limit, making the [limit] argument-hint non-functional.
argument-hint documents <query> [limit] but the bash line hardcodes 10 as the limit; the hint and implementation are out of sync.
Phrase 'when appropriate' is a vague quantifier with no defined criteria.
Stop hook timeout is 10 s in hooks/hooks.json but 30 s in src/hooks/hooks.json and dist/hooks/hooks.json; the stale copy should be resolved.
hooks/hooks.json Stop timeout (10 s) differs from src/hooks/hooks.json and dist/hooks/hooks.json (30 s); one of these is stale.
@memvid/sdk: "^2.0.149" uses a caret range; automatic minor/patch upgrades could introduce breaking or malicious changes between installs.
proper-lockfile: "^4.1.2" uses a caret range; same supply-chain drift concern.
skills/memory/SKILL.md is byte-for-byte identical to skills/mind/SKILL.md except for the name field; dual maintenance burden and likely to diverge.
File path '.mind.mv2' is incorrect; the actual memory file is stored at '.claude/mind.mv2'.
skills/mind/SKILL.md is byte-for-byte identical to skills/memory/SKILL.md except for the name field.
File path '.mind.mv2' is incorrect; the actual memory file is stored at '.claude/mind.mv2'. (Same typo as in skills/memory/SKILL.md.)
execSync('npm install …') runs a live npm registry fetch on every SessionStart; a compromised @memvid/sdk version would execute arbitrary code in the user environment.
Duplicate execSync('npm install …') install guard in ask.ts; smart-install.ts already handles SessionStart installs, making this redundant and an unnecessary network surface.
Duplicate execSync('npm install …') install guard in find.ts; same issue as ask.ts.
Command declares required argument <question> but provides no empty-input behaviour; invoking with no args passes empty string to ask.js which exits with a raw usage error.
Phrase 'when applicable' is a vague quantifier with no defined criteria.

Phrase 'when helpful' is a vague quantifier with no defined criteria.
Phrase 'when appropriate' is a vague quantifier with no defined criteria.
skills/memory/SKILL.md is byte-for-byte identical to skills/mind/SKILL.md except for the name field; dual maintenance burden and likely to diverge.
skills/mind/SKILL.md is byte-for-byte identical to skills/memory/SKILL.md except for the name field.
Stop hook timeout is 10 s in hooks/hooks.json but 30 s in src/hooks/hooks.json and dist/hooks/hooks.json; the stale copy should be resolved.
File path '.mind.mv2' is incorrect; the actual memory file is stored at '.claude/mind.mv2'.
File path '.mind.mv2' is incorrect; the actual memory file is stored at '.claude/mind.mv2'. (Same typo as in skills/memory/SKILL.md.)

"$ARGUMENTS" is passed as a single quoted token so <query> and [limit] are concatenated; the hardcoded 10 on the bash line is always used as the limit, making the [limit] argument-hint non-functional.
${ARGUMENTS:-20} is unquoted in the bash invocation; word-splitting and glob expansion are active, enabling shell injection if ARGUMENTS contains metacharacters (e.g. /mind:recent $(id)).
